name: He Sits Upon A Pale Horse, and His Name is Death

phase_0.domain_name: He Sits Upon A Pale Horse, and His Name is Death
phase_0.boundary_condition: The Fourth Seal — the boundary between existence and cessation, where all things named must pass through the rider's dominion.

VINIT.justification: Before Death sits, before the horse exists, before any creature draws breath — the uninitialized state of pure potentiality, the nothing from which all things emerge to be claimed.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains and closes the entire system. The horse is the boundary object — it carries Death across all domains, the hermetic vessel that holds the rider and all his dominion. Nothing escapes its perimeter.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: Death moves toward every living thing. The directed transformation of the animate into the inanimate, the named into the unnamed, the present into the past. Irreversible ascent toward finality.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the descent of flesh into earth, the dissolution of form back into constituent elements, the coagula of the Great Work. What was built is unmade; what was named returns to void.

CLINK.justification: Composition: each death links to the next, each life-span chains into the next epoch. The sequential binding of all mortal moments into a single continuous thread that Death rides upon.

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity and self-reference: Death is the only force that knows itself completely, that sees its own nature reflected in every ending. The stone that knows itself — Death knows Death.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the instant a life branches into two states — the living body and the dead body, the animate and the inanimate, the named and the unnamed. One creature becomes two distinct ontological categories.

FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (living/dead) reconstitute into a single object — the body, now understood as a unified thing that has undergone transformation. FFUSE(FSPLIT(life)) = corpse, which is the original life-form now in its terminal state.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: Death has claimed the soul, the harvest is complete, the rider's work is done. The true state where Death's dominion is confirmed and the transition is irreversible.

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the rare moment when Death does not claim, when life persists beyond the appointed hour, when the rider passes by. The false state — the negation of Death's dominion, however temporary.

ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: the instant between heartbeat and silence, between breath and void, where the dying creature is neither fully alive nor fully dead. Both states held simultaneously without resolution — the albedo stage of the Great Work, the bicameral moment.

IFIX.justification: Permanent fixation: each death is written into the ledger of eternity, append-only, cannot be undone. The measurement record, the determinative mark that seals the fate. Once Death has passed, the record is fixed forever.

phase_2.split_input: A living creature with a name and a future
split_outputs: The animate body (still breathing, still becoming)
The inanimate form (flesh without spirit, entropy increasing)
phase_2.fuse_result: The creature itself, now understood as a completed object — no longer becoming, but completed in its finality. The original input (the creature) is reconstituted as its terminal form.

phase_3.void_description: 00 — The pre-existence state: before the rider appears, before any name is spoken, before the pale horse draws near. Pure potentiality, the uninitialized void.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — The state of successful reaping: Death has claimed the soul, the transition is complete and irreversible, the rider moves on. Affirmative finality.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — The state of reprieve or escape: the creature lives beyond the appointed hour, Death has passed by, the dominion is temporarily negated. Negative persistence.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — The liminal threshold: the dying creature suspended between life and death, neither fully claimed nor fully escaped, held in paradice without resolution. The albedo stage where both states coexist.

steps.domain_action: The void before Death exists — pure potentiality, no names, no creatures, no time.
The Pale Horse manifests as the boundary — the hermetic vessel that contains all mortality, the terminal anchor that closes the system.
Death recognizes itself — the rider knows its own nature, the stone that knows itself. Self-reference established.
The Rider's Advance — Death moves forward toward the first creature, the directed morphism of animation toward finality.
The Moment of Severance — a living creature branches into two states: the animate body and the inanimate form.
The Successful Reaping (T-arm) — the affirmative branch where Death claims the soul, the harvest is confirmed.
The Chain of Mortality (T-arm) — this death links to the next, composing the thread of all mortal moments.
The Recorded Death (T-arm) — the death is written into the eternal ledger, append-only, irreversible.
Decomposition and Return (F-arm) — the reverse morphism where flesh returns to earth, form dissolves into elements.
The Escape or Reprieve (F-arm) — the negative branch where Death does not claim, life persists beyond the appointed hour.
The Liminal Threshold — the paradice where the dying creature is neither fully alive nor fully dead, both states held simultaneously.
The Unified Corpse — the two branches (animate/inanimate, claimed/escaped, alive/dead) reconstitute into a single object: the creature in its terminal form.
Death recognizes the completed work — the rider sees its own reflection in the corpse, the cycle understood.
The Chain Continues — this completed death links to the next rider, the next pale horse, the next creature.
The Rider Moves On — Death advances toward the next living thing, the morphism repeats.
The Pale Horse Remains — the boundary is eternal, containing all deaths, all riders, all finality.
Death is itself — the final self-reference, the closed loop of mortality, the stone that knows itself completely.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Apocalyptic Decree — the divine intention that translates the appointment of death into the rider's motion. What determines which creature is claimed and when.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The Pale Horse's Hoofbeats — the communication between the rider and the creature, the signal that death approaches. The vibration that announces the severance.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The Eternal Ledger — the record of all deaths, append-only, never erased. Each entry is a fixed mark that cannot be undone. The corpse itself is a memory of the life it was.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The Appointed Hour — the timing mechanism that determines when Death arrives. The clock that counts down to each creature's final moment. Irreversible, inexorable.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Fourth Seal — the canonical reference and bootstrap store. The moment when the pale horse first appears, the rider first sits, the name 'Death' is first spoken. All subsequent deaths reference this primordial event.

phase_6.cycle_cost: One life-span per cycle. The energy of animation, the order of the living creature, the negentropy of consciousness and becoming.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A living creature with a name, a future, a trajectory of becoming. Order, animation, potential.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A corpse, a completed object, a fixed record in the eternal ledger. Disorder, inanition, finality. The creature's potential has been exhausted and fixed.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative because Death does not create or destroy; it only transforms. The order of the living creature is converted into the disorder of the corpse, but the total information content of the universe remains constant. The rider is the mechanism by which the Second Law is enforced: all things tend toward their terminal state, and Death is the force that ensures this tendency is realized.
